description: Golden yellow lentils from the pigeon pea plant, fundamental to Indian cuisine. Toor dal is a staple protein source in vegetarian diets and is celebrated for its subtle, slightly sweet flavor and creamy texture when cooked.
flavorProfile: Subtle, slightly sweet, nutty, earthy, mild

nutritionalProperties.note: Plant-based iron; pair with vitamin C for better absorption

medicinalProperties.description: High fiber content helps slow glucose absorption, making it beneficial for diabetics. The low glycemic index makes it a sustainable energy source.
Rich in polyphenols and fiber. Regular consumption is associated with lower cholesterol levels and reduced cardiovascular disease risk.
Good source of resistant starch which feeds beneficial gut bacteria. Helps maintain healthy digestion and regular bowel movements.
Plant-based iron source essential for vegetarians. Pair with vitamin C-rich foods for enhanced absorption.
High protein and fiber content promotes satiety and helps maintain healthy weight.

storageTips: Store dried toor dal in an airtight container in a cool, dry place away from moisture and sunlight. Properly stored dal lasts 1-2 years. Keep away from strong spices as it absorbs flavors easily. If you notice any discoloration or off smell, discard immediately.
buyingTips: Choose clean, uniform golden-yellow dal without debris or discoloration. Avoid dal with an oily sheen (indicates age or poor storage). Buy from stores with high turnover for freshness. Organic, non-GMO toor dal is readily available. Check the date when purchasing—fresher dal cooks better and tastes superior.
